LanguageData.NewHSKEvents_HerdStay_LetterText: A herd of {0} has taken an interest in your colonists and settled nearby for a couple of days, curiously watching the settlers go about their lives.\n\nThey won't attack unless provoked. This is a good opportunity for hunting or taming.
LanguageData.NewHSKEvents_BirdAttack_LetterText: {0} has unleashed trained {1} on your colony! The birds are swooping in from three directions, diving at your colonists before regrouping for another strike.\n\nDefend the colony!
LanguageData.NewHSKEvents_StrawFire_LetterText: Someone left a piece of glass near the straw matting. The sun's rays reflected off it and ignited the dry straw!\n\nPut out the fire before it spreads!
LanguageData.HSK_CabinFeverDesc: Your animals have been cooped up indoors for too long! {0} of them have gone into a sudden rage, attacking everything in sight.\n\nLet your animals outside regularly to prevent this!
LanguageData.HSK_AnimalSnapDesc: {0} ({1}) suddenly became aggressive and attacked a nearby animal! Something must have set it off.\n\nKeep an eye on the situation.
LanguageData.HSK_CropBoostDesc: Thanks to your {1} tame animals, the soil has been naturally enriched with minerals from their manure. Your crops are thriving!\n\n{0} plants received a significant growth boost.
LanguageData.HSK_CropDeficiencyDesc: Your crops have begun to wither from a rare mineral deficiency! The soil lacks essential nutrients that are normally replenished by animal manure.\n\n{0} plants have died. You currently have only {1} tame animals — consider keeping more livestock to naturally fertilize the land.
LanguageData.NewHSKEvents_MineGasLeak_LetterText: A pocket of trapped gas has ruptured deep within the mountain! Toxic fumes are seeping into {0} underground rooms.\n\nEvacuate the affected areas immediately!
